steps.title: Translate the problem to equations
steps.content:
Let $p$ = pounds of potatoes and $c$ = pounds of celery.

From the problem statement:
  • Celery quantity: $c = 2p$ (twice as many pounds as potatoes)
  • Total cost equation: $0.69p + 0.99c = 5.34$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for pounds of potatoes
steps.content:
Substitute $c = 2p$ into the cost equation:
$$0.69p + 0.99(2p) = 5.34$$
Combine the $p$ terms:
$$0.69p + 1.98p = 5.34$$
$$2.67p = 5.34$$
Divide both sides by 2.67:
$$p = \frac{5.34}{2.67} = 2$$

steps.title: Calculate the pounds of celery
steps.content:
Using the relationship $c = 2p$ with $p = 2$:
$$c = 2(2) = 4 \text{ pounds}$$
Verification: $0.69(2) + 0.99(4) = 1.38 + 3.96 = 5.34$ ✓

That matches choice D — D is correct.
